card.title: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Unify phone, browser, and installed-app geometry so menu, play, Options, and Pause share measured safe content, status, overlay, motion, and fallback-background rules.
card.objective: Remove physical-device clipping, overlap, inconsistent sibling layouts, uncomfortable trail motion, fake scroll affordances, and purple safe-area bleed without introducing screenshot-specific offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play use one contained two-row status layout beside Pause at phone widths.
Options and Pause share guide, control-row, scroll, and pinned-action geometry.
Physical iPhone text remains inside status, guide, row, and button borders.
Page, app, Phaser, theme, and installed-app fallback backgrounds do not expose purple safe-area bands.
Fresh physical iPhone Safari and installed-PWA proof confirms the corrected release.
discoveries: PR #75 is merged and the corrected text-containment build is serving from canonical production.
Production 390x844 diagnostics passed all four surface families and both Pause scroll endpoints without containment or runtime errors.
nextActions: Collect fresh physical-iPhone Safari and installed-PWA screenshots from canonical production.
Complete only if device review confirms no clipping, overlap, border collision, purple bleed, sibling drift, or uncomfortable motion.
blockers: Fresh operator physical-iPhone proof of canonical production remains required.
evidence: Production deployment dpl_AZ9NUecXPHhn7PiWnXWjh9YoJm7u is READY at https://fawxzzy-mazer.vercel.app.
390x844 production diagnostics passed Menu, Options, Play, Pause, and both Pause scroll endpoints with zero guide, panel, button, containment, console, or page-error failures.

entry.headline: Physical-iPhone text containment correction is live in Mazer production
completed: Merged PR #75 at the exact reviewed head and deployed its merge commit to canonical production.
Verified Menu, Options, Play, Pause, scrolling, controls, buttons, and shared status geometry against the production asset.
Confirmed the canonical alias resolves to the READY production deployment.
discovered: Browser-level production diagnostics are clean; physical-device rendering remains the final external acceptance layer.
next: Keep the card in Review until that evidence is clean.

card.title: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive browser geometry so narrow layouts cannot overlap, escape, or replay stale dimensions after viewport changes.
card.objective: Recompute layout from the active content rectangle while reserving for physical-device text metrics and preserving only semantic user intent.
acceptanceCriteria: No viewport-derived geometry is persisted across incompatible browser states.
Menu, Options, play, Pause, and dialogs remain contained through supported viewport transitions.
Physical-phone glyph width and line height are represented by the shared fitting contract.
Native maximize, restore, browser-chrome, and phone evidence have deterministic expected outcomes.
discoveries: The layout-derived correction is live in production and persists no viewport geometry.
Production phone diagnostics passed all touched surfaces and Pause scroll endpoints without overlap, offscreen, or runtime errors.
nextActions: Collect fresh physical-iPhone proof from canonical production.
Extend the deterministic fixture to native maximize, restore, and browser-chrome changes.
blockers: Fresh physical-iPhone acceptance and native maximize/restore automation remain open.
evidence: Production deployment dpl_AZ9NUecXPHhn7PiWnXWjh9YoJm7u is READY at https://fawxzzy-mazer.vercel.app.
Production 390x844 diagnostics reported zero status, guide, row, button, panel, overlap, offscreen, console, or page-error failures.

entry.headline: Browser text-fitting correction is live without persisted geometry
completed: Deployed the responsive fitting correction to canonical Mazer production.
Verified production Menu, Options, Play, Pause, and scroll endpoint geometry at 390x844.
discovered: Production browser proof is clean; physical-device and native desktop window-transition proof remain distinct gates.
next: Run canonical production on the physical iPhone.
Retain native browser transition automation as the remaining persistence lane.
blockers: Physical-device acceptance and native transition proof remain open.

card.title: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one two-row status component in menu AI and active play for time, contextual rank, score out of 100, and maze level.
card.objective: Make menu and play progression information visually identical, readable, and contained while preserving the canonical score-data boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same status-panel layout and formatting contract.
Time, contextual Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze Lvl remain inside their phone and desktop borders.
The play panel fits its measured lane beside Pause without overlap.
Displayed score remains traceable to the canonical run-quality contract.
discoveries: The shared menu/play status correction is live in canonical production.
Production diagnostics report contained 252x60 menu and 248x60 play panels with textFits true.
nextActions: Review both production status contexts on a physical iPhone.
Confirm final score-field authority before terminal completion.
blockers: Fresh physical-iPhone proof and final canonical score-field traceability remain required.
evidence: Production deployment dpl_AZ9NUecXPHhn7PiWnXWjh9YoJm7u is READY at https://fawxzzy-mazer.vercel.app.
Production 390x844 diagnostics report a 252x60 menu panel and 248x60 play panel with textFits true, no overlap or offscreen findings, and zero console or page errors.

entry.headline: Shared menu and play status containment correction is live
completed: Deployed the shared compact panel reserve and physical-width safety ratio to canonical production.
Verified both production status contexts remain contained beside their sibling controls.
discovered: Browser production proof is clean; the physical iPhone remains the final rendering authority for the reported defect.
next: Collect fresh physical-iPhone menu and play screenshots.
blockers: Physical-device acceptance and score authority remain open.
